summarySeed.oneLiner: This page is for buyers who already know they need native AI video generation, but still need to pick the right lane: cinematic prompt-led clips, avatar-led business video, or fast social effects. Most bad shortlists happen when teams compare these lanes on headline price instead of publish-ready export path, watermark removal, usable credits, commercial-use posture, and how much governance the non-test tier actually provides.
boundaries: Native AI generation required: text-to-video, image-to-video, or avatar creation.
Excludes repurposing tools, transcript-led clipping tools, and editors that only add AI captions or filters without generating the core video.
Compared across workflow lane, publish-ready export path, watermark rules, credit economics, commercial-use posture, and governance path.
summarySeed.primaryClassificationRule: Group by the primary generation workflow the buyer is actually paying for.
comparisonAxes: usable credits or minutes on paid tiers

howToChooseSeeds.title: Choose the lane before you compare the brand.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Cinematic prompt-led generation (Sora, Runway), avatar-led communication (Synthesia, HeyGen, Elai.io), and effects-first social creation (Pika) are different product categories. Start by deciding which job you are paying for, or every later comparison will be noisy.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Price the tool by usable output, not advertised plan.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Most platforms sell credits, minute bundles, or gated export quality. Check how many publishable seconds or minutes the tier actually buys once you remove watermark restrictions and step up to the export quality you need.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Separate demo output from publish-ready output.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Free or lower tiers are often good enough to validate motion quality, but not to publish client work or internal rollout assets. Check whether the plan you can afford actually removes the watermark, unlocks 1080p or better exports, and keeps the workflow usable at real volume.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Check rights and governance before rollout.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: If the output is for paid campaigns, client delivery, or internal training at scale, verify the current commercial-use posture and governance path first. The practical difference between tools is often not raw generation quality but how clearly the paid tier supports real deployment.

groups.title: Cinematic generation
groups.rule: These tools are for prompt-first scene generation: cinematic B-roll, product visuals, and short clips where shot style matters more than presenter format. Choose this route if the core job is creating new footage, not delivering a speaking avatar or clipping existing source material.

groups.title: Avatar workflows
groups.rule: Platforms in this group center on AI presenters and speaker-led delivery. Use this route when the video needs a human-like narrator for training, onboarding, explainers, or multilingual communication, and compare based on minute bundles, stock-vs-custom avatar path, and governance depth.

groups.title: Short-form effects
groups.rule: This route is for rapid effects, motion gimmicks, and social-first clips where speed and iteration matter more than governance, training workflows, or long-form polish. Choose it when the output is meant to move fast on TikTok, Reels, or similar channels.

tools.name: Sora
tools.verdictSeed: Photorealistic text-to-video generation. Produces 15-second clips (up to 25s in Pro storyboard mode) with strong audio-visual quality.
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Commercial use typically requires a paid subscription

tools.name: Synthesia
tools.verdictSeed: Avatar-based video for corporate training and multilingual presentations, with stronger governance and export-policy clarity than many lighter avatar tools.
tools.pricingSeed: Freemium entry plus paid seat-based plans, with the publish-ready path starting on paid tiers
tools.policySeed: Freemium videos are watermarked, and clean exports require upgrading plus re-generating the video
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Commercial use comes with constraints around paid promotion using stock avatars, and some rollout controls such as SCORM or deeper governance stay on higher tiers

tools.name: Pika
tools.verdictSeed: Ultra-fast effects-driven content for TikTok and Reels via the Model 2.2 engine.
tools.policySeed: Paid plans explicitly add no-watermark downloads and commercial-use support, so free or lighter entry use is better treated as experimentation rather than a clean publishing path
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Cheap entry pricing still comes with credit ceilings, and heavier effect usage quickly pushes you toward higher tiers if you need reliable output volume

tools.name: HeyGen
tools.verdictSeed: Flexible avatar creation for multilingual presenter videos, outreach, and faster campaign-style experimentation.
tools.pricingSeed: Free entry plus paid creator and team paths, with cleaner publish-ready output on paid plans
tools.policySeed: Free output is best treated as a test lane, while paid plans are the practical path for watermark-free publishable delivery
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Usage is still governed by credits, scene limits, and plan-gated avatar minutes, so heavier rollout teams need to watch volume and governance fit

tools.name: Elai.io
tools.verdictSeed: A structured avatar-video platform for presenter explainers, presentation-to-video workflows, and multilingual business content without jumping straight to a heavier enterprise stack.
tools.pricingSeed: Free proof-of-concept lane plus paid self-serve and higher team tiers with 1080p-to-4K gating
tools.policySeed: Free is mainly a test lane; current official help positions the first paid self-serve tier as no-watermark 1080p, with broader team controls higher up the ladder
tools.bestForSeed: Presenter-led training, onboarding, and presentation-to-video workflows
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Self-serve usage still depends on minute bundles, and broad ownership or commercial-rights conclusions remain less settled than the export and workspace rules

tools.name: Runway
tools.verdictSeed: Creative control for effects-driven and cinematic short clips. Supports professional controls including color grading.
tools.policySeed: Free access keeps a watermark and excludes higher-end video access, while paid plans remove the watermark and unlock the cleaner publish-ready path
tools.bestForSeed: Creative effects and short cinematic clips
tools.mainLimitationSeed: The entry paid tier still runs on a 625-credit monthly cap, so heavier generation volume or higher-end models push you up the pricing ladder quickly

faqSeeds.question: What qualifies a tool for Best AI Video Generators (2026)?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: A tool qualifies only if it generates the core video natively through text-to-video, image-to-video, or avatar creation. This page excludes repurposing and transcript-clipping tools, plus editors that only add AI assistance around an existing timeline.

faqSeeds.question: What should readers compare first on this page?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Start with route fit: cinematic generation, avatar-led communication, or social-first effects. Then compare the publish-ready path on each candidate: watermark rules, export tier, and how many usable seconds or minutes the paid plan actually buys once you move beyond a demo.

faqSeeds.question: Are free plans enough to evaluate AI video generators?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Usually only for motion and interface testing. On this page, several tools keep watermark-free delivery, higher export quality, or cleaner commercial-use posture on paid tiers, so the paid plan structure matters more than whether a free lane exists at all.

faqSeeds.question: Which tools already have internal review coverage?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Current internal review coverage in this normalized dataset: sora, synthesia, pika, heygen, runway, elai-io.

reviewNotes: Elai.io was added from completed top-up and editorial handoff evidence to strengthen the avatar-workflow group.
Interactive pricing states were intentionally excluded in favor of workflow, export, watermark, and governance comparisons.
